${dtop('Capital Structure')}
v14 workbook

Capital Structure.

The v14 founding capital structure of 4orm Finance: a single-class Class A common cap table, the pre-seed unit offering, the team pool, vesting by holder, the Legacy Token concept, and how each choice benchmarks against the market.

${dmeta([['Version','v14'],['As of','2026-07-08'],['Room','Advisor'],['Class.','Confidential']])}
20,000,000
Founding Class A common shares
$10M
Pre-money valuation
$0.50
Unit price at pre-seed
$3.75M
Raise ceiling with milestone warrants

01Founding cap table

4orm Finance, Version 14, a single class of Class A common at one vote per share, dated 2026-07-06 with a v14 update on 2026-07-08.

HolderClassVoteShares%
Chad R. JohnstonClass A1:113,940,00069.70%
Kevin Wong (Co-Founder, Go-to-Market Strategy)Class A1:12,000,00010.00%
Bruce Fair (Founding Capital Markets Advisor via Mench)Class A1:1800,0004.00%
Sam Mraiheen (Founding contributor, passive)Class A1:1200,0001.00%
Don Hickling (Founding Angel)Class A1:1200,0001.00%
Miika Makela (Capital Strategy Advisor)Class A1:1200,0001.00%

James Atherton (Advisory Council seat)Class A1:150,0000.25%

Dean HesseClass A1:120,0000.10%

02Share class structure

ClassNotes
Class A common (1:1)Single class. All holders one vote per share. Includes Chad, Kevin, Bruce, Don, Sam, advisors, team pool, and any other holder.
Series Pre-Seed PreferredLegacy from v13 unit structure. v14 uses single-class Class A common at pre-seed (no preferred). Row retained for future series preferred use.
Founder voting bloc

Chad and Kevin hold a combined 79.70% of the vote at incorporation. In v14 this bloc is secured by the Founders Voting Agreement (the Voting Alignment Covenant); there is no voting trust and no proxy. The workbook's legacy "Voting Trust" label is superseded by the covenant approach.

03Pre-seed unit structure

$10M pre-money. $3M working target: $1.5M initial subscription, up to $3.75M with milestone-triggered warrants. Unit = 1 Class A common + 1/2 whole warrant at a $1.50 strike. Opens Aug 1, 2026.

Post-money valuation$11,500,000
Warrant triggers and architecture

Warrants are milestone-triggered (bank MOU, Alberta Sandbox acceptance, or Board-defined events). v14 uses single-class Class A common (no preferred at pre-seed). Corporate architecture: 4orm Finance (parent, Alberta ABCA) with operating subsidiaries stood up as regulatory milestones warrant.

04Team pool allocation

Pool = 12.00% = 2,400,000 shares Class A common. Version 11, aligned with the pre-seed standard of 10 to 15%.

05Vesting schedules

Monthly vesting with quarterly KPIs. Founders and team pool: 48-month monthly, 1-year cliff. Bruce, Miika, Angelo, Dean Hesse, Advisory Council: 24-month monthly, no cliff. Louise and Sam: fully vested at incorporation. Amounts are cumulative shares vested at the end of the period stated, with percentages of total shares.

06Legacy Token

Placeholder, subject to securities review. The concept is 2% of post-tax net income annually, distributed to Legacy Token pool holders indefinitely.

The v14 5-year model (with atomic settlement removed) shows approximately $101.8M revenue, approximately $83.1M pre-tax operating profit, and approximately $60.6M post-tax net income at a 27% blended tax rate. A 2% pool is approximately $1.21M distributable over 5 years: per-holder at 23 named holders is approximately $52.7K over 5 years; at a 40 pool cap it is approximately $30.3K per holder over 5 years. Securities review is required for the permanent distribution obligation.

23Dean McCallAdvisoryPlaceholder

Named holders occupy 23 of 100 slots; the remainder are open. Profit-share mechanic (concept):

  • Pool. 2% of the Company's net profit, calculated on an annual basis after audited financial statements.
  • Distribution. Pro-rata across all confirmed Legacy Token holders for that fiscal year (currently 1 of 100 is approximately 0.02% of net profit per holder; with 100 holders, approximately 0.02% each).
  • Term. Indefinite, subject to corporate continuation and counsel review on transferability and successor rights.
  • Eligibility. First 100 contributors who helped the Company prior to pre-seed close. Eligibility recognized in writing.
  • Separate from equity. Legacy Token participation is a profit-share recognition, not an equity grant. Holders may also separately hold equity on the Cap Table.
  • Securities classification. To be confirmed. Counsel to determine whether the Legacy Token is a security under Alberta and Canadian securities law (SOFI / Howey-equivalent). Distribution mechanics and any required prospectus exemptions to be confirmed.

07Benchmark matrix

Each element: the 4orm choice against a market benchmark, with an assessment flag.

Confidential. Internal capital-structure workbook, v14. Figures are drawn from the 4orm Finance capital structure workbook and are subject to definitive documentation, counsel review, and required regulatory approvals. Nothing in this document is legal, tax, or financial advice.

docs.fivemodel:
${dtop('5-Year Model')}
v14 Final Deployed

The 5-Year Model.

The full v14 financial model for 4orm Finance, read straight from the deployed workbook: five active revenue streams across four financial institutions, in semi-annual periods, all figures CAD.

${dmeta([['Version','v14 Final Deployed'],['Updated','2026-07-08'],['Room','Advisor'],['Class.','Confidential']])}
~$101.8M
Platform revenue, five years (CAD)
~$104.0M
Revenue with non-dilutive grants
~$83.1M
Pre-tax operating profit, EBITDA-equivalent, near 80% margin
~$60.6M
Post-tax net at 27% blended tax, near 59% net margin

01Five-year P&L

The model runs in semi-annual periods from the second half of 2026 through the end of 2031. The 2026 column reflects a half year only, and no revenue is booked until the first institution comes online in 2028. Values below are in CAD millions, and figures in parentheses are losses.

Line item (CAD M)2026202720282029203020315-yr total
Platform revenue--7.7022.9932.3338.79101.81
Non-dilutive grants-0.360.780.680.270.152.23
Total revenue + grants-0.368.4823.6732.6038.94104.04
DevEx (build costs)0.290.270.800.280.110.081.83
OpEx (ongoing)0.501.703.474.214.584.6819.13
Total expenses0.791.974.274.494.694.7620.97
Operating profit (pre-tax)(0.79)(1.61)4.2119.1827.9134.1883.07
Reading the margin

The operating profit line above is a pre-tax, EBITDA-equivalent figure: revenue plus grants, less development and operating costs, before financing and tax. On revenue plus grants of 104.04M, the 83.07M operating profit is a margin near 80%. That headline is an operating margin, not a net margin. Applying a 27% blended tax to the 83.07M gives roughly 60.6M in post-tax net income, a net margin near 59% on platform revenue. The model turns cumulatively profitable during 2028 after an early build period of losses.

02Revenue streams

Revenue is built from five active fee streams, each priced on a defined base and split with the partner institution. The 4orm share is the portion the platform retains. A sixth stream is reserved and is currently off, contributing zero in every period.

StreamFee rate4orm share5-yr revenue (CAD)
Settlement1.5 bps of settlement volume30%$2.82M
Issuance0.5% of issuance volume60%$39.32M
Trading5 bps of trading volume100%$2.62M
Custody30 bps of custody AUA40%$21.83M
SaaS + APIs$300,000 per enterprise client, per year80%$35.22M
Reserved 6th streamValue-based pricing on verified settlement savingsOff$0.00
Total$101.81M
Scope note

The Atomic Settlement Savings Share has been removed from scope. The workbook keeps a placeholder row for a reserved sixth revenue stream, but it is toggled off and carries zero across all periods, so it does not contribute to any figure on this page. Revenue is driven by four institutions coming online in sequence: ATB Financial and Bow Valley Credit Union in 2028, then Vancity Credit Union and Servus Credit Union in 2029.

03Non-dilutive capital

The model layers in grant and tax-credit income across the five years. This non-dilutive stack totals 2.23M CAD over the plan and flows into the revenue-plus-grants line above.

Program5-yr total (CAD)
Alberta Innovates FinTech Sandbox$250,000
NRC IRAP$500,000
SR&ED Tax Credits$430,000
Digital Supercluster$500,000
Innovative Solutions Canada$150,000
PrairiesCan$250,000
Mitacs$150,000
Alberta Enterprise Accelerate Fund IV$0
Total non-dilutive capital$2,230,000
Grant income by year (CAD M)2026202720282029203020315-yr total
Non-dilutive grants-0.360.780.680.270.152.23
How to treat it

The non-dilutive stack materially extends runway and reduces future dilution. It is best read as upside to the base plan and is not counted inside the $3M pre-seed use of proceeds. Most of these programs require documented eligible research activity in the tax year, so the larger claims build as the platform is developed.

04Method and margin

A short note on how the numbers on this page are constructed, so they can be read with confidence.

  • Periods. The model is semi-annual, spanning eleven half-year periods from the second half of 2026 through the second half of 2031. The 2026 annual column is a single half year.
  • Institutions. Four financial institutions drive revenue: ATB Financial and Bow Valley Credit Union onboard in 2028, Vancity Credit Union and Servus Credit Union in 2029. Each contributes across all five active streams.
  • Streams. Five active streams are live: Settlement, Issuance, Trading, Custody, and SaaS plus APIs. A sixth stream is reserved and switched off, and the Atomic Settlement Savings Share has been removed from scope.
  • Margin framing. The workbook's roughly 80% figure is a pre-tax operating, or EBITDA-equivalent, margin, not a net margin. Post-tax net at a 27% blended rate is close to 59% of platform revenue, or roughly 60.6M CAD on 83.07M of pre-tax operating profit.
  • Currency and rounding. All figures are CAD. Per-year values are shown in millions and rounded for readability, while the five-year totals reflect the workbook to the dollar.

Confidential. For information only. Figures are drawn from the 4orm Finance internal 5-year financial model, version 14 Final Deployed, and depend on assumptions that may not be realized, including the timing and scale of institutional onboarding, fee volumes, grant eligibility, and tax treatment. Post-tax figures apply an assumed 27% blended tax rate for illustration and are not tax advice. This document is not an offer to sell or a solicitation to buy any security, and nothing here is a promise or guarantee of revenue, profit, or value. Contains forward-looking statements subject to material risk; actual outcomes may differ.

${FT}
docs.capmemo:
${dtop('Capital Structure Memorandum')}
Cap table, control, unit structure, vesting

Capital Structure Memorandum

4orm Finance is being incorporated in Alberta under the Business Corporations Act (Alberta) as Canada's regulated settlement layer for tokenized bank deposits and cash-leg wedge infrastructure.

${dmeta([['Version','14'],['As of','2026-07-08'],['Room','Advisor'],['Class.','Confidential']])}

01Introduction

4orm Finance is being incorporated in Alberta under the Business Corporations Act (Alberta) as Canada's regulated settlement layer for tokenized bank deposits and cash-leg wedge infrastructure. This memorandum sets out the capital structure of the Company at incorporation: the founding cap table, the Control Preservation Framework binding the Founders, the pre-seed unit offering structure, and the vesting framework applied to each grant.

Version 14 supersedes v13 on raise size and unit structure. The founding cap table (v13) is unchanged. The pre-seed unit structure has been updated to reflect the confirmed terms: 3,000,000 Units at $0.50 per Unit for initial gross proceeds of $1,500,000, each Unit comprising one Class A common share and one-half whole warrant exercisable at $1.50 per share, with milestone-triggered warrant exercise. Full warrant exercise would generate up to an additional $2,250,000, for total potential capital of $3,750,000.

Key facts

  • Founding shares: 20,000,000 Class A common issued at $0.001 per share (founder tax-basis pricing).
  • Share class: single class of Class A common (1:1 voting) for all holders. No differential voting class.
  • Team pool: 12.00% of founding shares (2,400,000), reserved for non-founder hires within the pre-seed standard range of 10 to 15%.
  • Pre-seed offering: 3,000,000 Units at $0.50 per Unit at $10M pre-money valuation.
  • Unit composition: 1 Class A common share + 1/2 whole warrant (whole-warrant exercise price $1.50).
  • Warrant trigger: milestone-based (institutional MOU with a bank counterparty, Alberta Sandbox acceptance, or Board-defined events).
  • Total potential capital: $3,750,000 ($1,500,000 initial + up to $2,250,000 contingent on warrant exercise).
  • Standard shareholder protections: ROFR, pre-emptive, drag/tag, broad-based weighted-average anti-dilution.

02Founding Cap Table

20,000,000 founding common shares issued at $0.001 per share. All holders receive Class A common with a 1:1 voting multiple. The table below shows ownership at founding (pre-money).

HolderRoleShares% of Founding
Chad R. JohnstonFounder, CEO, President, Chairman13,940,00069.70%
Kevin WongCo-Founder, Go-to-Market Strategy2,000,00010.00%
Team Pool (unallocated)Reserve for non-founder hires2,400,00012.00%
Bruce Fair (via Mench Capital)Founding Capital Markets Advisor800,0004.00%
Sam MraiheenFounding contributor (recognition)200,0001.00%
Don HicklingFounding Angel200,0001.00%
Miika MakelaCapital Strategy Advisor200,0001.00%
Don Hickling (Council seat)Advisory Council50,0000.25%
James AthertonAdvisory Council50,0000.25%
Dean McCallAdvisory Council50,0000.25%
ZedAdvisory Council / Head Architect50,0000.25%
Angelo AquinoDomain Advisor (RWA)20,0000.10%
Dean HesseDomain Advisor (Research)20,0000.10%
Louise RoyerFounding contributor (recognition)20,0000.10%
TOTAL20,000,000100.00%

Founder holdings - combined Chad and Kevin bloc: 15,940,000 shares = 79.70% at incorporation. Kevin retains full economic and voting rights over his shares; there is no voting trust or irrevocable proxy. Coordination between the Founders is through the Voting Alignment Covenant in the Founders' Voting Agreement v14 §3.

Recognition-equity holders - Sam Mraiheen (1.00%) and Louise Royer (0.10%) hold founding recognition equity with no operational role. They are treated as ordinary shareholders and receive shareholder communications on the ordinary cadence.

03Pre-Seed Unit Structure

The pre-seed round is a unit offering at $10M pre-money valuation. The Company is offering up to 3,000,000 Units at $0.50 per Unit for aggregate initial gross proceeds of $1,500,000. Each Unit comprises one (1) Class A common share and one-half (1/2) whole warrant to purchase one (1) additional Class A common share at $1.50 per share. Warrants are milestone-triggered. Full exercise would generate up to $2,250,000 in additional gross proceeds, bringing total potential capital in to $3,750,000.

Pre-Seed Unit Economics

ComponentRatio / StrikePriceQuantityGross ProceedsDilution
Class A common share1 per Unit$0.503,000,000$1,500,00013%
Half warrant (whole strike $1.50)0.5 per Unit @ $1.50$1.501,500,000$2,250,00023%
Total potential capital$3,750,000

Pre-money share count 20,000,000. Post-share subscription 23,000,000 shares outstanding. Post-warrant exercise 24,500,000 shares outstanding. Implied post-money at share subscription $11,500,000. Implied post-money at full warrant exercise $36,750,000. Warrant triggers include an executed institutional MOU with a bank counterparty and Alberta Financial Regulatory Sandbox acceptance, and are being finalized by the Board.

04Control Preservation Framework

The Company preserves founder control through the following contractual mechanisms in the Founders' Voting Agreement v14 and Articles of Incorporation:

  • Voting Alignment Covenant (FVA §3): Founders consult before voting on Covered Matters and try to reach a common position; free will preserved for each.
  • Founder Consent Rights (Chad, FVA §5): eight enumerated matters requiring Chad's specific written consent while he holds at least 5% of outstanding shares.
  • Co-Founder Consultation Rights (Kevin, FVA §6): five enumerated matters requiring prior consultation with Kevin; sale of Company requires his written consent.
  • Phased Board Composition (FVA §7): 2 → 3 → 5 → 7 seats through Series A, with Chad as Chairman with tie-breaking vote at all stages.
  • CEO Tenure Protection (FVA §8): Chad removable only by 75% shareholder vote AND material cause.
  • Co-Founder Role Protection (FVA §9): Kevin removable from Board seat or role only by 75% shareholder vote AND material cause.
  • Supermajority Thresholds (FVA §11): 75% Articles / 90% Mission Anchor / 66.67% sale of Company, plus Founder Consent and Kevin Consent where applicable.
  • Mission Anchor Contractual Incorporation: incorporated by reference into all operative agreements; either Founder may invoke a Pause where a material Mission Anchor deviation is asserted.
  • Standard Economic Protections: ROFR, pre-emptive, anti-dilution, drag/tag per the Shareholders' Agreement.

05Vesting Framework

All founder and advisor share grants are subject to reverse vesting in the corresponding Reverse Vesting Agreement:

  • Founders (Chad, Kevin): 4-year monthly vesting with a 1-year cliff commencing on incorporation. 500,000 Kevin shares vest at the Cliff Date; the remainder vest monthly at approximately 41,667 shares per month for 36 months.
  • Bruce Fair (Founding Capital Markets Advisor): 24-month monthly vesting with secured-at-signing carve-out per the Mench Capital Advisor Agreement.
  • Advisory Council seats (Don, James, Dean McCall, Zed): 24-month monthly vesting with secured-at-signing carve-out per each Advisor Agreement.
  • Domain Advisors (Angelo, Dean Hesse), Capital Strategy Advisor (Miika): 24-month monthly vesting with secured-at-signing carve-out.
  • Founding contributors (Sam Mraiheen, Louise Royer): recognition equity; vesting per each individual grant letter.
  • Team pool (2,400,000 shares): granted from ESOP as employees are hired, subject to 4-year monthly vesting with 1-year cliff on individual grant terms.

Quarterly KPI Checkpoints are established by the Board for each Founder and reviewed each quarter (FVA v14 §10). Checkpoints are informational; they do not gate vesting.

06Team Pool and Hiring Roadmap

12.00% of founding shares (2,400,000 Class A common) is reserved for non-founder hires across the multi-entity Canadian regulated enterprise. Per-role allocations follow Carta hire-number medians for regulated fintech pre-seed. The team pool sits within the standard pre-seed range of 10 to 15%.

07Regulatory Framework

Securities: NI 45-106 accredited investor exemption for the pre-seed unit offering. NI 31-103 registration analysis on capital-raise compensation (per the Capital-Raise Compensation Policy; issuer-officer carve-out under CP 31-103CP s.1.3). The Alberta Securities Commission is the primary regulator; Osler (Alberta) and Fasken (BC) are external counsel.

Corporate: Alberta Business Corporations Act (ABCA). Articles of Incorporation incorporate the Mission Anchor by reference and set the single Class A common share class. Bylaws follow the standard Alberta corporate framework.

08Referenced Documents

  • Founders' Voting Agreement v14 - voting alignment covenant, Founder Consent Rights, Board Composition, CEO Tenure Protection, Mission Anchor stewardship
  • Co-Founder Agreement (Chad and Kevin) - individual co-founder relationship, roles, vesting, IP assignment
  • Reverse Vesting Agreements (each Founder and each Advisor) - vesting terms and clawback
  • Mission Anchor v14 - constitutional commitments
  • Decision Matrix v16 - operational authority, Board Approvals master list, Board Reporting Cadence
  • Capital Structure Workbook v14 (xlsx) - full cap table, benchmark matrix, pre-seed unit structure tab, vesting schedules
  • Equity Rationale v14 - position-by-position rationale for each holder's allocation
  • Capital Raise Compensation Policy v14 - compliance framework for capital-raise compensation
  • Mench Capital Advisor Agreement v14 - Bruce Fair's advisor engagement
  • Dean McCall Advisory Engagement Letter - CTO Placement Success Fee framework
  • Advisor Agreement Template v14 - standard advisor engagement
  • 5-Year Pro Forma v14 - financial model with SPEER toggle
${FT}

Strategy and fundraise status

Where we are right now.

The raise, the commitments, and the strategy, kept current. This is a living page, the figures update as they move.

01The raise

  • Round. $3M pre-seed at a $10M pre-money, opens August 1, 2026.
  • Commitments. Roughly $500K in LOIs and soft commitments, about $70K received to date.
  • Structure. Incorporation in Alberta and the subscription documents are in progress and nearly complete.

02The strategy

  • The wedge. The cash leg, a tokenized bank deposit, settling delivery-versus-payment with legal finality.
  • The MVP. Connect institutions first, then pull in tokenization projects that need our rails.
  • The build-up. Revenue from partners funds our own development team, tokenization engine, and smart-contract work.
Living document

Update the figures and status as they change; the live tracker is the Path to Revenue in the data room.

docs.legacy:
${dtop('Legacy Token Program')}
The first hundred

The first hundred.

Legacy tokens for the people who help build 4orm. Most people help build a thing and are forgotten. Not here. The first one hundred people who help get 4orm off the ground, paid or unpaid, whether they are with us for three days or three years, have their name added to a permanent list, and each receives ten legacy tokens. This is how we tie the people who mattered at the start into what 4orm becomes, for good, and pass it on to the people they love.

${dmeta([['Total pool','1,000 tokens'],['Contributors','First 100'],['Each','10 tokens'],['Profit share','2% net, yearly']])}
1,000
Legacy tokens in total
100
Founding contributors on the list
10
Tokens per person
2%
Of net profit, paid yearly to the legacy class

01How it works

  • Your name on the list. The moment you help, you are on it. Paid or unpaid, three days or three years, it does not matter. One hundred names, and no more.
  • Ten tokens each. One thousand legacy tokens in total, ten to each of the first hundred. That is the entire pool, allocated to the people who showed up first.
  • A share of the profit, every year. The legacy class is intended to receive 2% of 4orm Finance net profit annually, indefinitely, divided across the tokens and paid out yearly. Ten tokens is one percent of the pool, and at the scale 4orm is built for, it is designed to become a genuine inheritance over time, not a one-time bonus.

02Built to outlast you

  • Held in trust. We are working to place the legacy tokens in a trust, for tax efficiency and to protect the people who hold them.
  • Passed to your family. We are designing custody so tokens can be held securely and transferred to loved ones and family over time. A legacy in the real sense, not a slogan.
  • The list is real. It is created and tracked today. As the structure is approved, the list comes to fruition, name by name.
Where this stands

Pending securities and legal approval. The legacy token is in the research and discovery phase, being designed with counsel. This page describes our intent, not a finished instrument. It is not an offer, not a promise of payment or value, and the terms may change or may not proceed.

A first of its kind

To our knowledge, no company has built an instrument quite like this. We are in active compliance and legal discovery right now to structure it correctly, in a way that protects the people who hold these tokens, and their families, indefinitely. Getting that protection right comes before anything issues.

Confidential. For information only. This document describes a program under design and is not an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy any security, token, or other instrument, in any jurisdiction, and nothing here is a promise or guarantee of value, profit, distribution, or payment. The legacy token and any profit share, trust structure, custody, transferability, and tax treatment are conceptual, subject to securities and legal review, definitive documentation, and required regulatory approvals, and may change materially or may not proceed. Any profit-share percentage and payment cadence are intended terms only and are not committed. No securities regulatory authority has reviewed, assessed, or approved this program or any securities described here. Nothing in this document is legal, tax, or financial advice. Contains forward-looking statements subject to material risk; actual outcomes may differ. 4orm Finance is not a trust company and holds no trust authorization; any trust arrangement is subject to authorization and counsel review. Backstop: this program is all or nothing. If it does not proceed, or 4orm does not succeed, no legacy tokens issue, vest, or pay out, and no one receives anything, including the founders and every contributor, with no fallback, guarantee, buyback, or alternative compensation of any kind.

03The 2%, and what it could mean

A closer look at the legacy token, grounded in our model and cast forward.

Why the legacy token exists. The legacy token is a way of doing business differently. Some people come in early and, though they do not spend a lot of time with us, they truly move the business. This is how we honor the people who believed in us at the beginning. We believe relationships should last, so we tie those relationships into what 4orm becomes, for good, and pass that on to the people they love.

What the 2% is measured on. The legacy class is intended to share 2% of the Company's post-tax net income, paid yearly. "Profit" here means the bottom line: what is left after the business pays to build and run itself and after tax, the figure the audited statements report. We do not expect a profit in the early build years. In our model the Company is investing and running at a loss while it stands up the platform, and profit begins to build as the institutions come online. The honest way to read the 2% is to start the clock at the point the Company is profitable, and grow from there.

The anchor: our own model, over five years

~$60.6M
Post-tax net income over the 5-year model (v14)
~59%
Net margin, with about 80% pre-tax operating margin
~$1.21M
2% legacy pool over the five years
~$10.5K
Per 10-token holder, per year, at 23 named holders

Source: 4orm 5-year model (v14). Over the five years the model shows about $101.8M in platform revenue, about $104M including grants, and about $60.6M in post-tax net income at a 27% tax rate, a net margin of about 59 percent. The roughly 80 percent figure some documents cite is the pre-tax operating margin, not the net margin. Two percent of that post-tax net income is about $1.21M, distributed across the legacy pool over the five years.

Casting it forward: what the 2% shares out

Basis2% pool over five yearsPer holder over five yearsPer holder / year
23 named holders (the list today)~$1.21M~$52,700~$10,500
40-slot cap (if the pool is shared wider)~$1.21M~$30,300~$6,000

The per-holder figure depends on how many people ultimately share the 2% pool: about $52,700 each over the five years across the 23 named holders on the list today, or about $30,300 each if the pool is shared across a 40-slot cap. These figures sit on the five-year model only. If 4orm grows well beyond the five-year plan, and it is built to, the base the 2% is measured on grows with it, but we are not going to put a number on that here.

The idea, in one line

On our own five-year model, ten legacy tokens is designed to return on the order of several thousand dollars a year to the people who helped at the start. If the bigger vision lands, it becomes a genuine inheritance. That is what it means to honor a relationship that lasts.

Confidential. For information only. This document is illustrative and is not an offer to sell or a solicitation to buy any security, token, or instrument, and it is not a promise or guarantee of value, profit, distribution, or payment. Figures labeled as model output are drawn from the Company's internal 5-year financial model (v14) and depend on assumptions that may not be realized; the forward figures are illustrations, not forecasts, and carry very wide uncertainty. The legacy token, the 2% profit share, the basis on which profit is measured, and any trust, custody, transferability, and tax treatment are conceptual and subject to securities and legal review, definitive documentation in the Legacy Token Instrument, and required regulatory approvals, and may change materially or may not proceed. Any distribution, if the program proceeds, is calculated on the audited basis set out in the definitive instrument, which governs and may differ from the post-tax net income basis used here. Nothing in this document is legal, tax, or financial advice. Contains forward-looking statements subject to material risk; actual outcomes may differ. If the Legacy Token Instrument does not materialize, the allocation lapses and no substitute is owed.
